title: Hyperrectangle
description: Snuke received a d-dimensional hyperrectangle of size l1 × · · · × ld as a birthday present. Snuke placed it such that its i-th coordinate becomes between 0 and li, and ate the part of the hyperrectangle that satisfies x1 + · · · + xd ≤ s. (Here xi denotes the i-th coordinate). Let V be the volume of the part eaten by Snuke. We can prove that d!V (V times the factorial of d) is always an integer. Compute d!V modulo 109 + 7.
input: First line of the input file contains one integer d (2 ≤ d ≤ 300). Then d lines follow; i-th of these lines contain one integer li (1 ≤ li ≤ 300). Last line contains one integer s (0 ≤ s ≤ Σli).
hint: Illustration to Sample 1:
source: Contest, ICPC Japanese Alumni Group, JAG Summer Camp, JAG Summer Camp 2014 Day 2, Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Winter 2015, Day 2: Makoto Soejima Contest 1, Japanese Grand Prix, Contest, Open Cup, 2014/2015 Season, Stage 6: Grand Prix of Japan
